mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates overheating demand that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production signal immediate supply chain disruptions and earnings downgrade risks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates beyond 3.5%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.0 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer durables demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1000.0 fail to predict negative 5-day returns during housing downturns, or the signal produces false positives during periods of temporary weather-related construction delays.
